Document:
# Permanent Brig
Due to the uniquely dangerous nature of those sentenced to permanent confinement, the permanent brig operates on a separate set of standards and rules to the rest of the station.
Permanent brig inmates, up to the signing of this legislature, were all too free to engage without consequences due to a combination of their legal protections and a lack of some prisoners' fear for their own personal well being, but no more. Those who are legally sentenced to permanent confinement work on a "strike" system.
3 strikes and the prisoner may be DNR'd by any member of security charged with the containment of prisoners.
A signed paper detailing the reason and the proper storage of the body in a bodybag or morgue are both requirements to DNR outside an emergency.
Inmates with timed sentences MAY NOT be given strikes. Simply add to their sentence, reset their timer, or sentence them to permanent confinement.
## Prisoners are expected to:
-stand clear of their brigmates in the event they are attempting to commit or have committed a crime. Members of security are expected to and encouraged to treat anyone intentionally blocking line of sight or pathways as accomplices in any crime.
-comply with their sentence by staying inside their cell and a fair distance away from any points of escape.
-report the possession of/use of unapproved items, report attempts/stated plans of escape/other crimes by brigmates. Complicity
Any offense described below grants members of security the explicit right to enact lethal force on the offending parties.
## Prisoners are expected NOT to:
-Assault absolutely anyone. Assault grants one strike. Assault leading to death OR in spite of a member of security's clear verbal warning/command is 2 strikes. dangerous amounts of stamina damage (near stamcrit) is considered assault.
-Step outside their brig cell without security (not lawyer or outside command) approval for ANY reason other than their sentence having been served/commuted by approved staff. 2 strikes. - ## What is expected of brig staff: Though prisoners are still granted protections by space law before and after the altercation concludes, members of security are EXPLICITLY permitted to escalate to lethal force in two cases:
1: A prisoner is found outside of the brig or is actively fleeing the brig without communicated and approved (by the highest ranking member of security) parole. Empathy and common sense is still encouraged.
2: A prisoner commits any act of violence against station crew or visitor. This does not apply in instances of self defense.
## Parole: An inmate may be given clemency for their crimes and allowed to go free (parole) for the remainder of a shift. They require a new job and ID before being fully released without accompaniment from security or command staff.
This is a good opportunity for the parolee to work off extra brig time, but must be used with caution, as some prisoners are beyond rehabilitation. Use sensibility and the good judgement expected of station security when determining who can be released, be aware releasing known threats to the station without warning and proper measures in place may result in legal ramifications and dismissal from security positions. (Rolebans/shared perma sentences if consistent)
Parole is officiated by a stamped document from the head of security declaring parole, the individual paroled's name, their new role, and any terms of their parole (You may state ANY terms desired, with the writer being responsible for any violations or damages directly caused or implied by the terms. Parole is optional for both parties). If there is no head of security, a warden or captain's stamp will suffice.
A parolee must keep their parole document on them at all times, failure to show these papers on request by member of station security waives any rights granted by their new position and they may be freely apprehended and placed back in the brig for the remainder of their sentence.
## Treatment Of Prisoners Prisoners still have certain rights that must be upheld by law enforcement: - Prisoners must be granted adequate medical care as defined in the Bill of Rights up to the point they intentionally cause themselves harm. - Prisoners must be granted clothing, food, water, shelter and safety. If the brig is no longer safe, confinement must be established in another location. - Prisoners must be given access to legal counsel during an interrogation if requested and available. - Prisoners must be given their shift mandated PDA after confinement has finished, unless there is solid proof of PDA tampering. In case of tampering, the PDA is to be secured and replaced with a new unit. - Prisoners must be granted freedom of movement, and should not be restrained with handcuffs or other devices after incarceration unless there is an undue risk to life and limb. Similarly, any prisoners held for permanent confinement should be held in the communal brig, and should not be confined to a solitary cell unless they pose a risk to life and limb.
